mints.title: Adopt qdcli as the sole orchestration surface; delete spec-dag.mjs lifecycle commands and rewrite operating-model docs to teach qd
mints.spec:
qdcli became the orchestration ledger in commit 63f59ce (`feat(qd): adopt qdcli as orchestration ledger; import 639-node DAG`). The legacy node scripts/spec-dag.mjs lifecycle commands (claim, worktree, complete, ingest-audit, pop) and the justfile recipes (roadmap-pop, roadmap-ready) and the package.json scripts (roadmap:ready, roadmap:test, roadmap:issues, roadmap:validate) were not deleted at the same time. Docs/spec-dag.md, docs/orchestration-operating-model.md, docs/kaifuu-engine-playbook.md continue to teach the deleted-tool workflow (51 invocations of `node scripts/spec-dag.mjs ...` survive across docs/; ZERO docs reference qd). Per the standing rule `feedback_no_legacy_compat` (delete the old path in the same change as the new one — no shims, no dual plumbing), the legacy surface should be removed and the docs rewritten. The exception: `node scripts/spec-dag.mjs validate` may remain (it is the validator wired into `just check`) and `sync-issues` may remain (it is the GitHub-issue export pipeline that qd does not yet cover). Everything else goes.

Deliverables:
- Delete `claim`, `worktree`, `complete`, `ingest-audit`, `pop`, `ready`, `show`, `graph` subcommands from `scripts/spec-dag.mjs` (keep `validate` and `sync-issues`).
- Delete `roadmap-pop`, `roadmap-ready`, `roadmap-dashboard`, `roadmap-dashboard-watch` from the justfile if their qd equivalents cover the surface; otherwise rewrite them to call qd.
- Delete `roadmap:ready`, `roadmap:test`, `roadmap:issues` from `package.json` scripts.
- Rewrite `docs/spec-dag.md` (full rewrite — qd is the surface; spec-dag.json is the import source).
- Rewrite `docs/orchestration-operating-model.md` to make qd the canonical surface (not roadmap/spec-dag.json operations).
- Rewrite `docs/kaifuu-engine-playbook.md` §Worker workflow to use `qd ready / qd node show / qd node claim`.
mints.acceptance:
- `rg -n 'node scripts/spec-dag\.mjs (claim|worktree|complete|ingest-audit|pop|ready|show|graph)' docs/` returns zero hits.
- `rg -n 'roadmap-pop|roadmap-ready' justfile` returns zero hits (or the recipes are rewritten to call qd).
- `rg -n '"roadmap:ready|"roadmap:test|"roadmap:issues' package.json` returns zero hits.
- `docs/spec-dag.md`, `docs/orchestration-operating-model.md`, and `docs/kaifuu-engine-playbook.md` teach `qd ready`, `qd node show`, `qd node claim`, `qd complete`, `qd merge` instead of the spec-dag.mjs equivalents.
- `just check` still green (the surviving `spec-dag.mjs validate` invocation continues to work).
- `just ci` still green.
auditFocus: Doc rewrite that just sprinkles `qd` next to the old commands rather than deleting the old surface
Leftover justfile/package.json recipes that still call deleted spec-dag.mjs subcommands
Loss of the GitHub-issue sync pipeline (sync-issues should be preserved)
Loss of the `validate` CI gate (must survive)

mints.title: Replace KaifuuResult / UtsushiResult boxed-error aliases with typed enums or explicit boxing per call
mints.spec:
kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs:21 declares `pub type KaifuuResult<T> = Result<T, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;` and utsushi-core/src/lib.rs:125 declares the symmetric `UtsushiResult<T>`. 77 sites across the workspace use `Box<dyn std::error::Error>` directly. Meanwhile every engine-port crate (kaifuu-reallive, utsushi-reallive, kaifuu-vault-source) consumes `thiserror` (in workspace.dependencies as `thiserror = '2'`) and emits typed enums for parser, decode, encoding, and lifecycle errors. The cross-cutting smell is that typed errors stop the moment the call crosses a kaifuu-core / utsushi-core boundary, even though those crates host most of the API surface a downstream consumer sees.

This node is the inventory-and-decision pass, not a blanket rewrite. For each of the 77 sites:
  1. If the caller actually pattern-matches the error class, replace the box with a typed enum.
  2. If the caller bubbles unchanged, write `Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static>` explicitly at the boundary (no type alias) so the no-typed-error posture is grep-pinnable.
  3. If the boxed error is a deliberate `?`-friendly catch-all at a CLI boundary, keep it but mark the function `pub(crate)` if possible so it does not become public API.

Deliverables:
- An inventory artifact (markdown or jsonl) listing the 77 sites with their decision per site.
- The actual rewrite of the sites flagged as 'matchable', delivered in one or more chained nodes (this node is the planning / decision pass).
- Deletion of `pub type KaifuuResult` and `pub type UtsushiResult` once the matchable sites are converted (or a typed-error replacement alias if the boxed pattern is genuinely necessary).
mints.acceptance:
- `rg -n 'Box<dyn std::error::Error>' crates/` count decreases or carries a per-site rationale per site that remains.
- `pub type KaifuuResult` and `pub type UtsushiResult` are deleted, OR every surviving call site has a comment explaining why the boxed-error posture is correct at that boundary.
- All matchable sites are converted to typed enums.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` stays green.
- No new `panic!` / `unwrap()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
auditFocus: Conversion that silently widens an error type (e.g. wrapping a typed enum in a new `From<Box<dyn ...>>` impl, defeating the point)
Public-API breakage at the kaifuu-core / utsushi-core boundary that ripples into kaifuu-cli / utsushi-cli without an update
New `#[allow(deprecated)]` or `#[allow(unreachable_patterns)]` to mask half-converted boundaries
Loss of the `?`-friendly ergonomics at CLI boundaries

mints.title: Decompose crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs (23,110 lines) into module files matching the engine-port pattern
mints.spec:
crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs is 23,110 lines (17,135 production code + 5,975 in-file tests). The file hosts 229 top-level `pub fn / struct / enum / trait / mod` declarations. Compile time, IDE indexing, navigation, and refactor-impact analysis all suffer. The engine-port crates have adopted the `src/<module>.rs` layout (e.g., `utsushi-reallive/src/rlop/{mod, module_str, module_mem, module_sys, ...}.rs`); the core crates have not.

The decomposition can follow the precedent set by `scripts/apply-utsushi-146-decomposition.mjs` (the file that split the prior utsushi-reallive blob). Natural cut points:
  - `SEMANTIC_*` constants (currently 81 SEMANTIC_* string consts at the top of lib.rs) -> `src/semantic_codes.rs`.
  - `HelperRegistry*` types (HelperRegistryEntry, HelperRegistryError, fixture_helper_registry, register_entry, etc.) -> `src/helper_registry/`.
  - `KeyMaterial*` flow (key_import, key_validation, key_purpose, key_redaction) -> `src/key_material/`.
  - Bridge bundle plumbing (BridgeBundle, BridgeUnit, bridge_unit_id, contract validation) -> `src/bridge_bundle/`.
  - Profile + report builders (Profile, Report, profile_schema_version) -> `src/profile/`.
  - The 5,975-line embedded `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block -> `tests/` integration tests where possible, or a sibling `tests.rs` file at minimum.

Deliverables:
- A pre-decomposition inventory (markdown or jsonl) listing every top-level item and its target module.
- The actual decomposition committed as one logical change (no mid-flight broken builds; each landing commit must keep `cargo check --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, and `just ci` green).
- All public-API symbol paths preserved via `pub use` re-exports at the lib.rs root (no consumer break).
- No legacy-compat shim (per `feedback_no_legacy_compat`): the old monolithic lib.rs is fully replaced, not preserved in parallel.
mints.acceptance:
- `wc -l crates/kaifuu-core/src/lib.rs` drops below 2,000 (target: under 1,000; lib.rs becomes a module index + re-exports).
- Every public symbol that was previously importable as `kaifuu_core::<name>` still imports the same way (verified by `cargo check --workspace` plus an explicit re-export audit).
- `cargo test --workspace` is green.
- `just ci` is green.
- The 5,975 lines of test code are moved to `tests/` integration tests or `mod tests` siblings; the lib.rs body has no in-file test module.
auditFocus: Public-API breakage masked by `pub use *` (which would also leak private items)
Mid-flight commits that leave the workspace in a broken state
Legacy-compat re-exports that survive past the decomposition (no deprecation, no shim)
Test code that was moved without its `mod tests` setup (lost fixture helpers, lost `use super::*;` paths)
New circular module imports (the cores must remain a leaf of the workspace dep graph)
